name: Solomon
description: Solomon, son of King David and Bathsheba, reigned as the third king of Israel, succeeding his father and ushering in a golden age of peace and prosperity. He is renowned for his unparalleled wisdom, granted to him by God in a dream, which he famously demonstrated in the judgment of the two women claiming the same child. Solomon's reign was marked by ambitious building projects, most notably the magnificent First Temple in Jerusalem, which became the central place of worship for the Israelites. He established extensive trade networks, bringing wealth and exotic goods to the kingdom. However, his later years were marred by his accumulation of foreign wives, who led him to worship other gods, ultimately leading to the division of the kingdom after his death. Despite his flaws, Solomon remains a central figure in Jewish tradition, revered for his wisdom, justice, and the construction of the Temple, a symbol of God's presence among his people. His story serves as a cautionary tale about the dangers of pride and the importance of remaining faithful to God's commandments. His wisdom is also celebrated in the Book of Proverbs, Ecclesiastes, and the Song of Songs, texts attributed to him that offer insights into morality, philosophy, and love.
subtitle: Wisest of all men.
achievements: Judging the case of the two mothers

enemies.name: Jeroboam (leader of the northern tribes' revolt)

keyMyths.title: The Judgment of Solomon
keyMyths.description: Two women claimed the same baby was theirs. Solomon proposed cutting the baby in half, revealing the true mother's willingness to give up her claim to save the child's life.

keyMyths.title: Solomon's Dream
keyMyths.description: God appeared to Solomon in a dream and offered to grant him any wish. Solomon asked for wisdom to rule his people justly, pleasing God and earning him both wisdom and riches.

keyMyths.title: The Queen of Sheba's Visit
keyMyths.description: The Queen of Sheba, impressed by Solomon's wisdom and wealth, traveled to Jerusalem to test him with difficult questions, confirming his reputation and establishing trade relations.

sources.author: The Hebrew Bible
sources.work: 1 Kings, 2 Chronicles, Proverbs, Ecclesiastes, Song of Songs
sources.description: Primary source for the life and reign of Solomon.
sources.text: Primary source for the life and reign of Solomon.

sources.author: Flavius Josephus
sources.description: Historical account of Solomon's reign from a Jewish perspective.
